description: It is a great product. It has been very helpful. The team really enjoys the ease of use for the reps to complete the forms.
author: Robert B.
pros: It is very easy to use and create forms. The integration with Sharepoint works greats. The clients love the ease of completing the forms and it helps push the tasks to our field reps and frees up our Ops team to handle more jobs.
cons: An App for the Admin side to help dispatch jobs. Having the ability to have users in smaller groups but still see all the same forms. We have several reps where I don't want them to see ALL the reps but they need to see their Team members.

description: As long time users we have seen Pronto Forms develop many new features over the years and every time there is a change there is lots of tutorials, articles and support to learn and utilize the new features
author: Jen H.
pros: We have used Proto Forms for over 10 years to manage our field service teams and it has been very easy to use to all our staff including those with little to no experience with smart phone technology.
cons: The maps integration in our area is sorely lacking and as a result we have had to use several work arounds

description: Not fond of the system. It is time consuming to keep up with all the data being received by our users. I have to then transfer this data to another service to get some usable numbers that I can present to our executive group.
author: Glenn S.
pros: There aren't many positives if any I can say about True Context as it sits now. It is a data collection center. For an upcharge there are other analytics capabilities that other companies roll into one standard price.
cons: Smart logic works less than 50% of the time when you have long forms that require a lot of smart logic. The analytics you get is practically nothing. You have to build a power BI or another spreadsheet to decipher the data collection, so in theory, you are paying for multiple services that could be done with one provider. (Safety Culture and Predictive Solutions are two that are one-stop shops for data and analytics).
author_profession: Director of Safety Technology and Training

description: Robust form building tools and extensive background logic functions make building complex forms quite easy.
author: Steven S.
pros: User-friendly form building tools and adequate workflow configurations to aid in providing comprehensive form build options.
cons: Lack of true auto-save function. The platform could benefit from simpler methods for saving data to prevent loss of information in the field.

description: It covers my needs, but there are some things on my wish list. There is no API to connect to Power query so I cant send my submissions to an excel row, I can only set this up for Google Sheets.
author: Isaac G.
pros: Easy to use. I like that it saves files offline and eventually sends the report. On the back end, the API's are also a nice addition.
cons: Customer Support has no live line. Even when attempting to upgrade or change my account, there was a weeks wait time.

description: Overall very happy with the product. We think we are getting a lot of value considering how much we use it in our business.
author: Daniel F.
pros: The learning curve to getting started is great. The implementation of the truecontext university made it easy to onboard new members of the team with form building.
cons: Overall, our experience with TrueContext has been positive. However, we've encountered some issues—particularly with bugs and inconsistencies between the Windows and iOS apps. To improve accessibility and ensure a more consistent user experience across platforms, we would strongly prefer the availability of a web-based version rather than relying on the Windows app.

review_title: Good app for tracking technicians tasks on site.
description: Overall it is a great app, very useful for tracking issues with technician tasks on site. As they perform a task they complete the checklist and the issues are traceable.
author: Bianca M.
pros: It is a very useful tool for tracking issues with technician tasks on site. As they perform a task they complete the checklist and the issues are traceable.
cons: When creating forms, the numbering of questions and destinations are not automatic. It would save my team a lot of time if they were automatically numbered.

review_title: TruContext is a user-friendly form generating software.
description: Overall, our experience with TruContext has been positive and has increased efficiency, tracking and accountability.
author: Marcella B.
pros: The software is very easy to use and set up forms. We utilize the software for a multitude of departments, for example safety and quality audits, vendor material and lot tracking, and process flow documentation. Having the ability to utilize conditional formatting, take pictures with notation and export the data has helped us improve our processes.
cons: The biggest hurdle we have had is setting up workflow for our quality audits. We use one form to document quality defect corrections and only one employee can work on the form at a time. It would be beneficial to have the ability to have more than one user be in a form and see updates in real time.

description: Overall positive. The program can be complicated to learn without taking significant time to create custom forms and fix mistakes. However, I have not seen another program that has near the capabilities of TrueContext. Relationship with support has been positive.
author: Travis P.
pros: Customizable form questions and conditional logic, and the various data destinations that are not all on other platforms. Great program for large, complicated forms.
cons: Form Builder is a bit clunky and needs updating. Some bugs with large forms, and some issues lately with email data destinations blocking certain emails within our user domain.

review_title: Reliable Digital Forms with Room for Workflow Improvements
description: TrueContext has helped us move away from paper-based workflows while reducing errors in data collection. It standardizes our forms and makes sure technicians capture the right information each time they’re in the field. The automated workflows and instant data uploads save us time and strengthen collaboration between field teams and the office. Overall, it makes our field processes faster, more reliable, and much easier to manage day to day. However, the workflow can feel convoluted at times, and some options aren’t always clearly visible or easy to access when I need them.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Creating and editing forms with a wide range of options works very well. Collaboration features are solid as well. The initial learning curve can be somewhat steep, but understanding the basics can get you a long way to using TrueContext effectively. Overall, the forms significantly simplify field work.
cons: The workflow can feel convoluted at times, and some options aren’t always clearly visible or easy to access when I need them.

review_title: A lot of potencial but still a lot to improve
description: Overall it is a good tool but for sure it has a lot to improve. There are basic features or options that are no possible to use yet, but I believe it can easily be implemented.
author: Fábio R.
pros: The amount of input types that can be used in the forms. The possibility to link it with Salesforce Servicemax. The fact that it has sections and sub sections for the forms.
cons: Limited use of the templates. Does not allow to change the resource image used in the template. Same resource image for every question using the template. Too many steps to create a resource image. Lack of options to apply some settings to multiple objects.

review_title: Automation of Mobile workflows and app building management through ProntoForms !
description: My overall experience with ProntoForms is best till now. It is one of the best mobile solutions for workflow streamlining along with app building in most strengthened manner so that a strong, scalable and replicable work flow and process management solution can be built around without any problem. Managing all ground operations activity and capturing of evidences from field team is now easy task and does not pose any issues in collecting primary and secondary data for businesses. Visualizing data through real time dashboards and bringing business intelligence in raw data is possible with ProntoForms .
author: Frinto A.
pros: The best mobile forms solution, available in the market for businesses and professionals. The strong app building management through this forms to create robust forms for field team and ground team. All the complex work tasks and work activities are managed in unified manner and can be monitored through single workflow. The ground team can take accurate input from field in these forms in effortless manner without any hustle. Pronto Forms is the one stop destination for contextualizing and customizing Health, Safety and Management aspect of training management in most easy manner possible. No trouble at all.
cons: We are still figuring out for deploying this software in offline manner without internet connectivity. Most of our ground teams operate in remotest districts of the states and does not have any internet bandwidth or network connectivity in such regions. Real time submission of filled forms should be made available in offline manner without internet usage. Cost reduction can be further looked into for devising new low cost strategy model on usage of the ProntoForms at lowest cost possible in different business environments.

description: Good Experience - used it for our customers for the last 3 years implementing across UK NHS. I like how flexible the tool is.
author: Jack H.
pros: I like how you can connect so many different data sources and destinations to a form. And how it can produce multiple different doc types and DREL/handlebar documents are so easy to configure.
cons: It's hard to build forms for non technical users. Not as intuitive as other platforms available. We struggle showing customers how to make their own forms, we have to always create them.

description: Excellent platform. Easy to use Easily manageable No coding can be learned by anyone in short time.
author: Geethanjali T.
pros: Learning Curve Value for Money Customer Support Bugs and Issues Workflow Management Form Customization Project Management
cons: Limited Implementation Need to have lots of flexibility feature Need to have the feasibility to check errors on user level in web portal

review_title: TrueContext - best on site tool for technician
description: Easy to build new forms. Easy to obtain data from the field and feed other systems. Great connections to external datasources and destinations
author: Tomasz F.
pros: Easy to build new forms and connect data with other systems. Great integration with Sharepoint and others. Good customer support - fast reaction and extremely good problem solving. Output documents customization (with Handlebars)
cons: When working with bigger forms it becomes slightly slower while editing/adding/removing questions (minor issue).

description: We started with a big learning curve as I had to help the tech team redesign a couple of forms. Once the technicians had their login credentials and learned the form submission, it really sped up billing and the response time to customers.
author: Candace M.
pros: Our company was able to move to paperless invoicing and form submissions. As a subcontractor, we only had access to certain features but it definitely improved our response time to customers.
cons: Older style website version, had to download an extension to help download invoioces quicker than one at a time.

review_title: Robust Platform, but Onboarding Needs to be More Beginner-Friendly
author: Diego R.
pros: he best part is the seamless integration with our existing systems. The ability to automatically generate reports and send them to the cloud in real-time has improved our workflow tremendously
cons: feel that the learning curve is a bit steep. The support materials and guides tend to skip over basic steps, assuming the user is already familiar with the logic of the system. This makes it less beginner-friendly than other tools I've used.

description: Overall it is a decent platform with plenty of features that can be applied in numerous ways, depending on user's creativity. Low entry threshold allows for immediate start and first simple deployment in a matter of minutes. For advanced users however, it is also not difficult to quickly hit the limits of TrueContext which can bring a bit of a frustration. Nevertheless, we mostly managed to build what we needed, one way or another.
author: Pawel C.
pros: Very easy to implement, start with the design and deploy first form. Superb documentation - with a bit of patience it can solve most of the issues. Great API and decent amount of connectors for painless integrations.
cons: Conditional logic might be quite annoying and requires a lot of workarounds to achieve desired outcome. Customer support rarely solves those more challenging problems. Still many features are missing like e.g. ability to dispatch attachments that could be submitted with the form.

description: It's been a very short period of time working with TrueContext, but I think that the overall app has been great to work with as an admin and it's easy to learn new things about it due to the documentation.
author: Alex S.
pros: The documentation is very solid, makes it very easy to work on creation of users and starting an SSO/connecting an API key.
cons: I think the overall application is very solid, I haven't worked with it long enough to say anything other than not liking the UI.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Easy powerful forms. TrueContext is for every company!
description: Happy with the functionality. We look forward to future updates.
author: Tina W.
pros: Easy to build forms and if support is needed, the team at True Context/Pronto Forms are very helpful. Our company is happy using this system for all our form submissions!
cons: We are unable to add logos/diagrams to our forms unless we pay extra fees. This should be a standard. For example, the health and safety area. Why can't we add logos for people to understand better instead of having to read everything.

description: My overall experience has been wonderful. I find it useful and easy to use on the go!
author: David T.
pros: I like how it is an app and easy to use on a mobile platform.
cons: I feel building the forms can be made easier.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Experience has been great. There is an endless amount of possibilities with True Context.
author: Maverick M.
pros: The seamless ease of use within the mobile app.
cons: The difficulty when integrating between box. There is no feature to plot all submissions on a map simultaneously.

description: Truecontext is a great app that really allows me to see any issues on the job from my home office
author: Latonja H.
pros: It allows me to see details of the jobsite in real time.
cons: The team cannot share notes with each other and it only allows one person to upload notes.

review_title: Most secure and manageable productivity app
description: Since started using ProntoForms, I could start offering end users a great variety of new fields they need to document and report. It's easy to manage and everything is secure.
author: Uriel P.
pros: Ease of management and use, and the great Support by Support Team.
cons: App layout for Mobile users, some of them fail to find important fields.

author: Tiago A.
pros: the capability of using different devices such IOS, Android...
cons: It´s a tool that still works very slow and make us lose more time than it should.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
